description_long: This object is a classic portable radio receiver with a rectangular body, featuring a prominent black handle on top for easy carrying. The front face displays a large circular speaker grille on the right, a cassette tape deck on the left, and a series of control buttons and dials above, including a tuning scale and frequency indicator. The top surface has a set of tactile buttons, likely for controlling playback and radio functions, as well as a visible telescopic metal antenna extending upward for signal reception. The sides of the radio include input/output ports and a large rotary knob, likely for tuning or volume adjustment. The exterior is primarily composed of black and gray plastic with metallic accents, giving it a robust and utilitarian appearance. This radio receiver is typical of those found in urban environments, suitable for use in public spaces, garages, or outdoor gatherings.
description: A classic portable radio receiver with a black handle, large speaker grille, cassette deck, control buttons, and a telescopic antenna. The body is mainly black and gray plastic with metallic details.
description_view_0: Front view showing the speaker grille, cassette deck, control buttons, and frequency indicator.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the thickness of the radio and a large rotary knob.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying a plain back panel with the handle and antenna visible.
description_view_3: Opposite side view showing input/output ports and the edge of the top control panel.
